In Parliament

  • Tweet

You can find all my formal contributions to debates in Parliament here. I am working hard both inside and outside the House of Commons chamber to represent the interests of Sevenoaks & Swanley residents - so if there is something you’d like me to raise, please get in touch by emailing [email protected] - please be sure to include your full name and address. 

Special Educational Needs: Finance | Department for Education | Written Answers

To ask the Secretary of State for Education, whether the £4bn announced in her Department's policy paper entitled Every child achieving and

Special Educational Needs: Finance | Department for Education | Written Answers

To ask the Secretary of State for Education, whether the £3.5 billion funding announced in the White Paper entitled Every child achieving an

After Clause 26 - Power to require internet service providers to prevent or restrict access by children to internet services | Children&#8217;s Wellbeing and Schools Bill | Commons debates

My right hon. Friend is always correct to be worried, but he will know that in Lord Nash’s amendment a 12-month delay was written in.

After Clause 26 - Power to require internet service providers to prevent or restrict access by children to internet services | Children&#8217;s Wellbeing and Schools Bill | Commons debates

My right hon. Friend is always correct to be worried, but he will know that in Lord Nash’s amendment a 12-month delay was written in.

After Clause 26 - Power to require internet service providers to prevent or restrict access by children to internet services | Children&#8217;s Wellbeing and Schools Bill | Commons debates

My right hon. Friend is always correct to be worried, but he will know that in Lord Nash’s amendment a 12-month delay was written in.

After Clause 26 - Power to require internet service providers to prevent or restrict access by children to internet services | Children&#8217;s Wellbeing and Schools Bill | Commons debates

On Friday, after Labour MPs had repeatedly blocked a social media ban for under-16s, we had a new proposal from the Government.

After Clause 26 - Power to require internet service providers to prevent or restrict access by children to internet services | Children&#8217;s Wellbeing and Schools Bill | Commons debates

I would be incredibly concerned if that were the case.

After Clause 26 - Power to require internet service providers to prevent or restrict access by children to internet services | Children&#8217;s Wellbeing and Schools Bill | Commons debates

As ever, the hon. Gentleman is correct. I am sure that will be picked up by the Minister in her closing remarks.

After Clause 26 - Power to require internet service providers to prevent or restrict access by children to internet services | Children&#8217;s Wellbeing and Schools Bill | Commons debates

My right hon. Friend, as ever, is absolutely correct. As on the smartphone ban in schools, we have been fought every step of the way.

After Clause 26 - Power to require internet service providers to prevent or restrict access by children to internet services | Children&#8217;s Wellbeing and Schools Bill | Commons debates

I would be incredibly concerned if that were the case.
